title: Anatomy of a Probability Vector
description: A flowchart illustrating the definition, constraints, and role of a probability vector within a Markov Chain.
elements: Next State Probability Vector (v_{next} = v * P)
relationships: A Probability Vector is defined over a State Space, where each element pi is the probability of being in state si.
The vector must satisfy two constraints: all elements are non-negative (pi ≥ 0) and their sum is 1 (Σpi = 1).
In a Markov Chain, the current probability vector is multiplied by the transition matrix P to calculate the probability vector for the next state.
